content:
# Ruth Chapter 4 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), page 882 | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/RUT_004_page_001.jpg

---

**4:1** And-Boaz went-up [to] the-gate, and-he-sat-down there. And-behold, the-kinsman-redeemer [go'el] [was] passing-by, of-whom Boaz had-spoken. And-he-said, "Turn-aside, sit-down here, so-and-so" [peloni almoni]. And-he-turned-aside, and-he-sat-down.

**4:2** And-he-took ten men from-[the]-elders of-the-city, and-he-said, "Sit-down here." And-they-sat-down.

**4:3** And-he-said to-the-kinsman-redeemer [go'el], "The-portion of-the-field which [was] to-our-brother, to-Elimelech, has-sold Naomi, the-[one]-returning from-[the]-field of-Moab.

**4:4** And-I said, 'I-will-uncover your-ear,' saying, 'Buy [it] before the-[ones]-sitting and-before the-elders of-my-people. If you-will-redeem, redeem. And-if he-will-not redeem, tell to-me, and-I-will-know, for there-is-none except-you to-redeem, and-I [am] after-you.'" And-he-said, "I will-redeem."

**4:5** And-said Boaz, "On-[the]-day of-your-buying the-field from-[the]-hand of-Naomi, and-from Ruth the-Moabitess, [the]-wife of-the-dead, you-buy [it], to-raise-up [the]-name of-the-dead upon his-inheritance."

**4:6** And-said the-kinsman-redeemer [go'el], "I-am-not-able to-redeem for-myself, lest I-ruin my-[own]-inheritance. Redeem for-yourself, you, my-right-of-redemption, for I-am-not-able to-redeem."

**4:7** And-this [was] formerly in-Israel concerning the-redemption and-concerning the-exchange, to-confirm every matter: a-man drew-off his-sandal and-gave [it] to-his-neighbor; and-this [was] the-attestation in-Israel.

**4:8** And-said the-kinsman-redeemer [go'el] to-Boaz, "Buy [it] for-yourself." And-he-drew-off his-sandal.

**4:9** And-said Boaz to-the-elders and-[to]-all the-people, "Witnesses [are] you today that I-have-bought all which [was] to-Elimelech, and-all which [was] to-Chilion and-Mahlon, from-[the]-hand of-Naomi.

**4:10** And-also Ruth the-Moabitess, [the]-wife of-Mahlon, I-have-acquired for-myself for-a-wife, to-raise-up [the]-name of-the-dead upon his-inheritance, and-[the]-name of-the-dead will-not be-cut-off from-with his-brothers and-from-[the]-gate of-his-place. Witnesses [are] you today."

**4:11** And-said all the-people who [were] in-the-gate, and-the-elders, "[We are] witnesses. May-YHWH-give the-woman the-[one]-coming to your-house like-Rachel and-like-Leah, who built, the-two-of-them, the-house of-Israel. And-do valiantly in-Ephrathah, and-call [a]-name in-Beth-lehem.

**4:12** And-may-your-house be like-[the]-house of-Perez, whom Tamar bore to-Judah, from the-seed which YHWH will-give to-you from this young-woman."

**4:13** And-Boaz took Ruth, and-she-was to-him for-a-wife, and-he-went-in to-her. And-YHWH gave to-her conception, and-she-bore a-son.

**4:14** And-said the-women to Naomi, "Blessed [is] YHWH who has-not-left to-you [without] a-kinsman-redeemer [go'el] today. And-may-his-name be-called in-Israel.

**4:15** And-he-shall-be to-you for-a-restorer of-life, and-to-sustain your-old-age, for your-daughter-in-law who loves-you has-borne-him, who she [is] better to-you than-seven sons."

**4:16** And-Naomi took the-child, and-she-placed-him in-her-bosom, and-she-was to-him for-a-nurse.

**4:17** And-called to-him the-neighbor-women a-name, saying, "A-son has-been-born to-Naomi." And-they-called his-name Obed. He [is the]-father of-Jesse, [the]-father of-David.

**4:18** And-these [are the]-generations of-Perez: Perez begot Hezron.

**4:19** And-Hezron begot Ram, and-Ram begot Amminadab.

**4:20** And-Amminadab begot Nahshon, and-Nahshon begot Salmah.

**4:21** And-Salmon begot Boaz, and-Boaz begot Obed.

**4:22** And-Obed begot Jesse, and-Jesse begot David.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **4:1 -- peloni almoni** (פְּלֹנִי אַלְמֹנִי): Literally "so-and-so" or "a certain one." The nearer kinsman-redeemer is never named in the text. This deliberate anonymity is ironic: the man who refused to "raise up the name" of the dead has his own name erased from the narrative.

2. **4:3 -- "has sold"** (מָכְרָה): The perfect tense is used. Whether Naomi has already sold the land or is about to sell it is debated. The Leningrad Codex vocalization supports a completed action, and EMET renders it accordingly.

3. **4:4 -- "he will not redeem"**: The Ketiv reads לֹא יִגְאַל (3rd person, "he will not redeem") while the Qere reads לֹא תִגְאַל (2nd person, "you will not redeem"). The shift between 2nd and 3rd person is characteristic of the Leningrad Codex text here. EMET follows the Ketiv.

4. **4:5 -- Ketiv/Qere**: This verse contains a significant textual issue. The Ketiv reads קָנִיתִי ("I have bought" -- Boaz speaking of himself) while the Qere reads קָנִיתָ ("you buy/acquire" -- addressed to the go'el). The difference fundamentally affects the legal transaction described. EMET follows the Qere reading as it fits the narrative logic of the gate scene.

5. **4:7 -- Sandal custom**: The narrator provides an antiquarian note explaining a custom already archaic by the time of writing. This editorial aside is one of the clearest indicators that the book was composed at a temporal distance from the events described.

6. **4:10 -- "wife of Mahlon"**: Ruth is here identified as Mahlon's wife. In 1:4 the text does not specify which son married which woman. This verse clarifies that Mahlon (not Chilion) was Ruth's husband.

7. **4:11 -- Rachel and Leah**: The order "Rachel and Leah" (not the birth-order "Leah and Rachel") places the beloved wife first. Both matriarchs are invoked as builders of Israel's house.

8. **4:12 -- Perez and Tamar**: The blessing invokes another story of a woman (Tamar) who took initiative to preserve a family line through a Levirate-like arrangement (Gen 38). The parallel to Ruth is deliberate.

9. **4:17 -- "A son has been born to Naomi"**: The neighbor-women attribute the son to Naomi, not to Ruth. This completes the reversal from 1:21 ("empty") to fullness. The child is named Obed (עוֹבֵד, "serving/worshipping").

10. **4:18-22 -- Genealogy of David**: The book concludes with a ten-generation genealogy from Perez to David. Ten generations is a conventional number in biblical genealogies (cf. Gen 5, Gen 11). This toledot formula (וְאֵלֶּה תּוֹלְדוֹת) connects Ruth to the Pentateuchal genealogical framework.

11. **4:20-21 -- Salmah/Salmon**: The Leningrad Codex writes שַׂלְמָה (Salmah) in v. 20 but שַׂלְמוֹן (Salmon) in v. 21 for what appears to be the same individual. This variant spelling is preserved as found in the codex.

-- EMET
